result:
Security audit complete. Here's the summary.

## Harden: Security Audit — Complete ✅

**Entry point:** `./` · **Outcome:** PR · **PR:** https://github.com/thabungm/testHubLaunch/pull/49

### What I scanned
The repo is a shell-script project: 8 helper scripts in `.github/scripts/`, the 3,345-line `ralph-run.sh`, and config files (`.vscode/settings.json`, `.gitignore`, `skills-lock.json`, `.agents/skills/*`). I checked OWASP Top 10 categories, hard-coded secrets, insecure defaults, injection sinks, and dependency vulnerabilities.

### Findings & fixes

| Severity | Issue | Location | Status |
|----------|-------|----------|--------|
| **High** | Insecure temp file — predictable name in world-writable `/tmp` (CWE-377/CWE-59): symlink-overwrite + PR-diff info disclosure | `hula-verify-gather.sh:116` | ✅ Fixed |
| **High** | Same class; also leaked on `die()` error paths before cleanup | `hula-verify-post.sh:63` | ✅ Fixed |
| Info | `eval "$CMD"` on regression commands | `ralph-run.sh:2977,3044` | By design — trusted, repo-controlled config; not changed |

**Fix:** both temp files now use `mktemp` (unpredictable name, `0600`, private dir); `hula-verify-post.sh` gained a `trap … EXIT` so the transient comment file is removed on all exit paths.

### Verification (per completion criteria)
- `bash -n` syntax check passes on all 9 scripts (no lint/type-check tooling exists for a shell repo; shellcheck not installed).
- `mktemp` pattern functionally tested — confirms `0600` perms + unpredictable suffix + clean removal.
- **No dependency manifest exists** (`package.json`/lockfiles absent), so `npm/pnpm audit` is not applicable — no dependencies means no new critical findings introduced.
- Reviewed credential handling in `ralph-run.sh`: tokens read from env, used only in auth headers, never logged; webhook payloads JSON-escaped; skill downloads HTTPS-only. No hard-coded secrets.

The minimal, targeted fixes are committed on `harden/security-audit-20260701` and the PR is open.
